SOURCE 2: ANONYMOUS - LAYOFF → COACHING CONSULTANCY (HIGHEST STAKES)
Rating: 9/10 ★★★★★

URL: https://fairygodboss.com/career-topics/why-getting-laid-off-turned-out-to-be-the-best-thing-for-my-career
Publication: Fairygodboss
Date: Updated April 30, 2020
Word Count: ~900 words

---NARRATIVE ARC---

BEFORE: 
- Director of Organizational Development
- Nearly 5 years building department from ground up
- Left "one of the best companies I'd ever worked for" for this job
- "Very high-achieving, career-focused woman"
- Age 42, just back from maternity leave (2 months)
- Had terminated many people throughout career, never been let go herself

TRIGGER: "We had a meeting about your department and have some bad news. For you anyway. Today's going to be your last day."

CRISIS:
- NEWBORN TWINS (just had) + 2-YEAR-OLD
- Just contracted LIVE-IN NANNY (in preparation for return to work)
- Husband started gym that year → MAKING $0 (she was sole earner)
- "With that single call, we went from excited and comfortable to QUALIFYING FOR FOOD STAMPS"
- "Didn't have a lot of coping skills at that time for handling stress and pressure"

NIGHTMARE IMAGERY:
- "Being jobless was the stuff of nightmares"
- "Very short walk, in my mind, from top of my game to LIVING OUT OF A SHOPPING CART"
- "In my nightmares, it was always a shopping cart"

EMOTIONAL COMPLEXITY:
- Hurt, angry, afraid
- Tempted to pursue LEGAL ACTION against former employer
- "Emotional rollercoaster" (twins + toddler + job loss)
- Had to reconcile self-image as "successful"
- Discovered: "truly believed I didn't define myself by my role or paycheck, but once they were gone, it was tough"

DECISION POINT: "Decided to use whatever energy I had to move forward not backward" (chose not to sue)

HIDDEN DREAM: 
- Dream/passion for YEARS: work for self as coach, consultant, writer
- Starting gym was strategy to replace her income so she could leave corporate, spend time with kids, enter new profession
- "Would I ever have done it? I'd like to think so, but who knows?"
- "We were comfortable enough to make change distasteful"

BREAKTHROUGH: "Getting laid off with no notice was the SHOVE OUT OF THE NEST I probably needed to take a risk on myself"

AFTER:
- "Thriving coaching and leadership consultancy"
- Set own pace, hours, rate
- Work with people she enjoys
- Spend time with kids ("time I always wanted")
- "More wisdom, knowledge, professional connections, and opportunities than I've ever had"
- "Wouldn't trade where I am now for the world"
- Stronger marriage: "walked through that together and emerge stronger on the other side"

---5 LESSONS LEARNED---
1. GIFT OF DESPERATION: Shove out of nest to risk on self
2. FACE MY FEARS: "Worst thing I could imagine, professionally, and I survived" / "Will never tolerate being mistreated by an employer again"
3. GAINED EMPATHY: "Makes me a better coach" - having been there
4. DISCOVERED WHO I WAS: Identity beyond role/paycheck
5. DOING WHAT I LOVE: Thriving consultancy, time with kids

---EMOTIONAL BEATS (15+)---
1. "Worst fears were realized"
2. Left best company for this (double betrayal)
3. Just back from maternity leave (2 months)
4. Newborn twins + 2-year-old
5. Contracted live-in nanny
6. Husband's gym: $0
7. Sole breadwinner
8. "Qualifying for food stamps"
9. "Shopping cart" nightmare (repeated)
10. "Very short walk from top of my game"
11. "Didn't have coping skills"
12. Hurt, angry, afraid
13. Legal action temptation
14. "Emotional rollercoaster"
15. Self-image crisis
16. Forward vs. backward choice
17. "Shove out of nest"
18. "I survived"
19. Marriage strengthened
20. "Thriving consultancy"
21. Time with kids
22. "Wouldn't trade where I am now"

---ESL RESONANCE MULTIPLIERS: 6/6---
✓ Overcoming "Not Good Enough" (jobless = failure in mind)
✓ Building New Life (consultancy from scratch)
✓ Persistence (walked through fear with family)
✓ First-Time Courage (entrepreneurship despite no coping skills)
✓ Connection Across Differences (marriage strengthened, empathy for clients)
✓ Belonging (created thriving practice, finds joy in work community)

---STORY POWER---
HIGHEST EXTERNAL STAKES: 3 kids under 3, food stamps, sole earner, nanny contract
FINANCIAL CRISIS VISCERAL: Shopping cart nightmare imagery unforgettable
SERVICE PIVOT: Corporate → helping others (coaching/leadership)
FAMILY DIMENSION: Marriage strengthened, time with kids achieved
LEGAL COMPLEXITY: Considered suing but chose forward energy
IRONIC EXPERTISE: Terminated others, never experienced it herself
